message:
feat(vc_collect): thread asupersync::Cx through Collector trait for structured concurrency (bd-3lu)

Add `&asupersync::Cx` as a required parameter to the `Collector::collect()` trait
method, enabling structured cancellation, checkpoints, and budget enforcement
for every collector task. This is the foundational wiring for the bd-3lu
structured concurrency work.

Changes:
- Extend `Collector::collect()` signature: add `cx: &asupersync::Cx` parameter
  before the existing `ctx: &CollectContext` parameter
- Update all 13 collector implementations (afsc, beads, caam, cass, caut,
  cloud_bench, dcg, github, mcp_mail, ntm, pt, rano, rch, sysmoni) plus
  5 test/dummy collectors (DummyCollector, IncrementalDummyCollector,
  ToolRequiringDummyCollector, BatchDummyCollector, RuCollector,
  FallbackProbeCollector) to accept the new parameter (prefixed `_cx`
  since none use it yet)
- Thread `Cx` through `MultiMachineCollector::collect_all()` and
  `collect_from()` in remote.rs, cloning it per-task for fan-out
- Thread `Cx` through `collect_local()` internal helper
- Enrich tracing spans: log machine_count, max_concurrent, cancel_requested
  at region entry; update region exit message to "all tasks drained"
- Add `crossbeam-deque` dependency to Cargo.lock (transitive)
- Add structured concurrency tests in lib.rs:
  - Compilation test for Cx in trait signature
  - Three-collector all-succeed fan-out test
  - Three-collector one-fails isolation test
  - Cx cancellation visibility test
  - Zero-collector empty region test
  - Cx Clone verification test
- Update all existing tests to construct `Cx::for_testing()` and pass it

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
